title: Tabular Data Metadata
description:
Tabular dataset metadata.

This metadata schema provides a structured framework for defining and describing tabular data files. It enables clear, standardised documentation of file identity, integrity, and structure, as well as detailed characterisation of each field within the dataset. The schema captures essential information needed to interpret fields or variables, ensure their consistent definitions, assess data suitability,  and support harmonisation across datasets. This schema is intended to support reproducibility, interoperability, and transparent documentation, enabling downstream validation, dataset comparison, and integration into broader analytical workflows.

Complete all applicable fields based on the available documentation and knowledge of the dataset.

files.title: Files
files.description:
Tabular data files with homogeneous structure and format.

**Constraints**: *Maximum number* of files: 100,000.

items.title: File
items.description: A file path with format, checksum, and optional title and description.

path.title: Path
path.description:
A relative file or directory path from the root directory.

**Constraints**: Must include only letters, numbers, underscores, hyphens, forward slashes, or periods. Parent directory references (../) are not allowed. *Maximum length*: 255 characters.

format.title: Format
format.description:
The file's format such as PDF, TSV etc., or user-specified format.

**Constraints**: Must contain only letters, numbers, hyphens or underscores. *Length*: 1-30 characters.

sha256.title: SHA256 Checksum
sha256.description: The SHA256 checksum of the file.

label.title: File Label
label.description:
Human-readable file label.

**Constraints**: Must contain only letters, numbers, or underscores. *Maximum length*: 255 characters.

title.title: Title
title.description:
A short, descriptive, user-friendly file title, with each word capitalised.

**Constraints**: *Maximum length*: 255 characters.

description.title: Description
description.description:
A brief description of the file's contents.

**Constraints**: *Maximum length*: 5,000 characters.

examples.title: Data Quality Issues
examples.description: Known data quality issues in the dataset.
SNOMED CT codes used to construct the variables in the dataset.

fields.title: Fields
fields.description:
List of fields or variables in the tabular data.

**Constraints**: *Maximum number* of fields: 100,000.

items.title: Field
items.description:
A field or variable within a tabular data file.

**Use**: Used to document each field or variable so users can interpret values, validate data types, and align variable semantics across datasets.

**Guidelines**: Complete as many relevant sections as feasible. Set to `unknown` or `not applicable` as appropriate where supported.

label.title: Label
label.description:
A short, stable, user-friendly identifier.

**Use**: Used in scripts, interfaces, logs, and cross-references.

**Guidelines**: Prefer PascalCase. Spaces are not allowed. The label should not be the description or definition of the field.

**Constraints**: Must contain only letters, numbers, or underscores. *Length*: 2-128 characters.

**Examples**: `SystolicBloodPressure` | `HeartFailure`

description.title: Description
description.description:
A concise definition.

**Use**: Used in data dictionaries and field listings to clarify the field's meaning and context.

**Guidelines**: Describe what the field refers to, measures, or encodes.

**Constraints**: *Maximum length*: 5,000 characters.

dataType.title: Data Type
dataType.description:
The data type of the values.

**Use**: Used to interpret values and apply type-specific validation and processing.

**Guidelines**: Choose the type that matches how the values are stored. Boolean values should be encoded as 0/1 and represented as integers.

**Constraints**: Must be one of `integer`, `float`, `date`, `datetime`, or `string`.

dateFormat.title: Date/Datetime Format
dateFormat.description:
String format for `date` and `datetime` values.

**Use**: Used to interpret the source date or timestamp representation.

**Guidelines**: Provide the format pattern used for encoding date/datetime values. Prefer ISO 8601 format. Set to `not applicable` for non-date/datetime fields.

**Constraints**: *Maximum length*: 64 characters.

**Examples**: `YYYY-MM-DD` | `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS`.

measurementType.title: Measurement Type
measurementType.description:
The measurement type or scale of the field.

**Use**: Used to determine how the variable should be interpreted and handled in data visualisation, processing, and analysis.

**Guidelines**:
- Categorical fields: use `nominal` for unordered categories, `ordinal` for ordered categories, `binary` for two-category fields.
- Numeric fields: use `discrete` for countable values, `continuous` for uncountable, or measurable quantities.
- Set to `unknown` if the measurement type cannot be determined.
- Set to `not applicable` for fields without a measurement type such as subject identifiers.
- Set a custom measurement type if none of the above apply.

**Constraints**: Must be one of `nominal`, `ordinal`, `binary`, `discrete`, `continuous`, `unknown`, `not applicable`, or custom string. *Maximum length*: 32 characters.

anyOf.title: Select
anyOf.description: Select from the list of the canonical measurement types.

anyOf.title: Custom
anyOf.description: Enter a custom measurement type; use this only if the canonical list doesn't contain an appropriate entry.

role.title: Intended Role
role.description:
The intended analytical or usage role.

**Use**: Used in modelling and data processing to distinguish outcomes, predictors, and supporting fields.

**Guidelines**:
- Use `target` for outcomes to be predicted, `feature` for predictors, `auxiliary` for fields used to derive outcomes or features, `index` for indexing fields such as identifiers.
- Set a custom role if none of the above apply.
- Set to `unknown` if the intended role cannot be determined.

**Constraints**: Must be one of `target`, `feature`, `auxiliary`, `index`, or a custom string. *Maximum length*: 32 characters.

anyOf.title: Select
anyOf.description: Select from the list of standard roles.

anyOf.title: Custom
anyOf.description: Enter a custom role; use this only if the standard list doesn't contain an appropriate entry.

categories.title: Categories
categories.description:
A list of unique categories for a nominal, ordinal, or binary field.

**Use**: Used in data dictionaries and field listings to clarify the categories' meaning and context.

**Guidelines**: Provide only for categorical fields. Each item should include a label and an integer code. Set to `not applicable` for non-categorical fields. Set to `unknown` when the labels of the integer-coded categories are unknown.

**Constraints**: *Number of categories*: 1-10,000. No duplicates.

items.title: Category
items.description: A single category of a nominal, ordinal, or binary field.

label.title: Label
label.description:
Clear and informative category label.

**Constraints**: *Length*: 1-255 characters.

code.title: Code
code.description: Integer code used in data files instead of the label.

allOf.title: Tags
allOf.description:
A list of unique, essential tags.

**Use**: Used in semantic alignment, search and filtering.

**Guidelines**: Tags should represent the key domain or descriptive aspects of the thing being tagged, based on its definition and content. If no appropriate standard concept is available, use a clear word or short phrase. Avoid overly general or redundant tags.

**Constraints**: *Maximum number* of tags: 10.

anyOf.title: Concept Code
anyOf.description:
A concept code used as a tag.

**Use**: Used for semantic alignment, search, and filtering.

**Guidelines**: Ensure the concept code is semantically correct and comes from a standardised vocabulary or coding system.
- `code` must be a valid identifier from the specified vocabulary.
- `name` must match the preferred name of the concept in the specified vocabulary.
- `code` and `name` must be matching pairs within the specified vocabulary.
- `vocabulary` should be a stable, recognisable identifier (for example, a domain vocabulary or code system name).
- `vocabularyVersion` should be stated when possible.

**Constraints**:
- `code`: a string or integer.
- `code`: *maximum length*: 255 characters if a string.
- `name`: *maximum length*: 255 characters.
- `vocabulary`: *maximum length*: 75 characters.
- `vocabularyVersion`: *maximum length*: 75 characters.

code.title: Code
code.description: A concept code from a standardised vocabulary or coding system.

name.title: Name
name.description: The preferred concept name associated with the code.

vocabulary.title: Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: The vocabulary or coding system associated with the code.

isPrimary.title: Primary
isPrimary.description:
Indicates whether this concept is the primary or most representative tag.

**Guidelines**: Only one concept should typically be marked as the primary tag. Leave unset if no primary concept can be determined.

confidenceScore.title: Confidence Score
confidenceScore.description:
Confidence in concept representativeness.

**Guidelines**: Confidence score assigned by a tagging model or system. Provides a measure of certainty for automatically generated tags. Only include this field for tags generated by an automated process.

**Constraints**: *Range*: [0.0, 1.0].

name.title: Name
name.description:
A user-friendly tag name.

**Guidelines**: Keep it short, descriptive, and unambiguous. Avoid acronyms unless widely recognised.

**Constraints**: *Maximum length*: 255 characters.

isPrimary.title: Primary
isPrimary.description:
Indicates whether this tag is the primary or most representative tag.

**Guidelines**: Only one tag should typically be marked as the primary tag. Leave unset if no primary tag can be determined.

confidenceScore.title: Confidence Score
confidenceScore.description:
Confidence in tag representativeness.

**Guidelines**: Confidence score assigned by a tagging model or system. Provides a measure of certainty for automatically generated tags. Only include this field for tags generated by an automated process.

**Constraints**: *Range*: [0.0, 1.0].

units.title: Units
units.description:
A measurement unit string as defined by the Unified Code for Units of Measure (UCUM).

**Guidelines**: See https://unitsofmeasure.org/ucum. Set to `not applicable` if not applicable. Set to `unknown` if the unit is unknown.

anyOf.title: UCUM
anyOf.$comment: The schema does not enforce conformance to UCUM; this should be validated by the application.
anyOf.description:
A measurement unit string as defined by the Unified Code for Units of Measure (UCUM).

**Guidelines**: See https://unitsofmeasure.org/ucum.

**Constraints**: *Maximum length*: 255 characters.

missingValueCode.title: Missing Value Code
missingValueCode.description:
A single code used to represent missing values.

**Use**: Used to parse missing values.

**Guidelines**: Use either an integer or a short string; string values may include letters, numbers, forward slashes, underscores, hyphens, or spaces. Set to `not applicable` if the field has no missing values. Set to `unknown` if missing values exist but the missing value code is unknown.

**Constraints**: *Maximum length* for string codes: 25 characters.

fieldType.title: Field Type
fieldType.description:
Special field type indicator.

**Use**: Used in downstream processing of special fields.

**Guidelines**: Use `standard` for variables to be used directly in analysis or variables used to derive targets or features, `subject_id` for subject identifiers, `baseline_indicator` for binary baseline flags (1 = baseline, 0 = follow-up), `record_datetime` for record or observation date/time, `temporal_order` for integer time order derived from observation timestamps, especially when actual observation timestamps are unavailable or cannot be provided, and `path` for file or directory path fields. Use a custom entry for special field types not listed here.

**Constraints**: Must be one of `standard`, `subject_id`, `baseline_indicator`, `record_datetime`, `temporal_order`, `path`, or a custom entry. *Maximum length*: 128 characters.

anyOf.title: Select
anyOf.description: Select from the list of the pre-specified field types.

anyOf.title: Custom
anyOf.description: Enter a custom field type; use this only if the pre-specified list doesn't contain an appropriate entry.

tags.title: Field Tags
tags.description:
Tags associated with the field.

**Use**: Used in data discovery and semantic alignment of fields from different datasets.

**Guidelines**: Set to `not applicable` for fields that do not represent variables such as dates. Set to `unknown` if the tags were not specified but could be in principle.

anyOf.title: Tags
anyOf.description:
A list of unique, essential tags.

**Use**: Used in semantic alignment, search and filtering.

**Guidelines**: Tags should represent the key domain or descriptive aspects of the thing being tagged, based on its definition and content. If no appropriate standard concept is available, use a clear word or short phrase. Avoid overly general or redundant tags.

**Constraints**: *Maximum number* of tags: 10.

anyOf.title: Concept Code
anyOf.description:
A concept code used as a tag.

**Use**: Used for semantic alignment, search, and filtering.

**Guidelines**: Ensure the concept code is semantically correct and comes from a standardised vocabulary or coding system.
- `code` must be a valid identifier from the specified vocabulary.
- `name` must match the preferred name of the concept in the specified vocabulary.
- `code` and `name` must be matching pairs within the specified vocabulary.
- `vocabulary` should be a stable, recognisable identifier (for example, a domain vocabulary or code system name).
- `vocabularyVersion` should be stated when possible.

**Constraints**:
- `code`: a string or integer.
- `code`: *maximum length*: 255 characters if a string.
- `name`: *maximum length*: 255 characters.
- `vocabulary`: *maximum length*: 75 characters.
- `vocabularyVersion`: *maximum length*: 75 characters.

code.description: A concept code from a standardised vocabulary or coding system.

name.title: Name
name.description: The preferred concept name associated with the code.

vocabulary.title: Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: The vocabulary or coding system associated with the code.

**Constraints**: *Range*: [0.0, 1.0].

**Constraints**: *Maximum length*: 255 characters.

Indicates whether this tag is the primary or most representative tag.

Confidence in tag representativeness.

**Guidelines**: Confidence score assigned by a tagging model or system. Provides a measure of certainty for automatically generated tags. Only include this field for tags generated by an automated process.

**Constraints**: *Range*: [0.0, 1.0].

resources.title: Resources
resources.description:
A list of URL links or file references related to measurement or construction.

**Use**: Provides supporting documentation such as codebooks, protocols.

**Guidelines**: Use URL links or file references. Set to `not applicable` if there is no further information on the field. Set to `unknown` if relevant resources exist but cannot be retrieved or shared.

**Constraints**: *Maximum number of items*: 1,000. No duplicates.

anyOf.title: Resources
anyOf.description: External resources:  URL links or file paths.

anyOf.title: URL Link
anyOf.description: Uniform Resource Locator (URL) with a title and optional description.

url.title: Link
url.description:
A valid HTTP or HTTPS URL (Uniform Resource Locator).

**Constraints**: *Maximum length*: 2,048 characters.

title.title: Title
title.description:
A short, descriptive, user-friendly URL title, with each main word capitalised. 

**Use**: Used in interfaces for display purposes instead of the actual URL link. 

**Guidelines**: Use specific, meaningful keywords to indicate what the URL is about at a glance. 

**Constraints**: *Maximum length*: 255 characters.

description.title: Description
description.description:
A brief description of the URL's content. 

**Guidelines**: Be clear and concise.

**Constraints**: *Maximum length*: 1,500 characters.

examples.title: UCUM Units
examples.description: Measurement units defined by Unified Code for Units of Measure (UCUM).

anyOf.title: File
anyOf.description: A file path with format, checksum, and optional title and description.

A relative file or directory path from the root directory.

**Constraints**: Must include only letters, numbers, underscores, hyphens, forward slashes, or periods. Parent directory references (../) are not allowed. *Maximum length*: 255 characters.

format.title: Format
format.description:
The file's format such as PDF, TSV etc., or user-specified format.

**Constraints**: Must contain only letters, numbers, hyphens or underscores. *Length*: 1-30 characters.

sha256.description: The SHA256 checksum of the file.

label.description:

**Constraints**: Must contain only letters, numbers, or underscores. *Maximum length*: 255 characters.

title.title: Title

**Constraints**: *Maximum length*: 255 characters.

description.title: Description
description.description:
A brief description of the file's contents.

**Constraints**: *Maximum length*: 5,000 characters.
